examples.title: Algorithm: Working Set Model for Thrashing Prevention
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the Working Set Model approach to prevent thrashing by only allowing processes with their working sets in memory to run
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WorkingSetModel
INPUT: List of processes, total_frames available, time_window τ
OUTPUT: Set of processes allowed to run

BEGIN
 FOR each process p IN active_processes DO
 p.working_set ← CalculateWorkingSet(p, τ)
 p.frame_count ← CountFrames(p)
 END FOR

 available_frames ← total_frames
 running_processes ← EMPTY LIST
 suspended_processes ← EMPTY LIST

 // Sort processes by priority (higher first)
 SortProcessesByPriority(active_processes)

 FOR each process p IN active_processes DO
 IF available_frames >= p.working_set.size THEN
 // Process fits in memory with its working set
 Add p TO running_processes
 available_frames ← available_frames - p.working_set.size
 ELSE
 // Not enough frames - suspend process
 Add p TO suspended_processes
 Suspend(p) // Swap out entire process
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN running_processes
END

// Function to calculate working set using τ (time window)
FUNCTION CalculateWorkingSet(process p, τ)
 pages_referenced ← EMPTY SET
 current_time ← GetCurrentTime()

 FOR each page_ref IN p.page_reference_log DO
 IF (current_time - page_ref.time) < τ THEN
 Add page_ref.page TO pages_referenced
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN pages_referenced
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The Working Set Model prevents thrashing by ensuring each process has enough frames to hold its current working set (pages referenced within time window τ). If total demand exceeds available frames, processes are suspended. This maintains CPU utilization by avoiding thrashing.

examples.title: Python: Thrashing Simulation and Detection
examples.description: Python simulation demonstrating how thrashing occurs and implementing Page Fault Frequency detection to prevent it
examples.code:
"""
Thrashing Simulation and Detection in Operating Systems
Demonstrates: Thrashing conditions, Page Fault Frequency (PFF) monitoring
"""
import time
import random
from collections import deque

class Process:
 def __init__(self, pid, num_pages, allocated_frames):
 self.pid = pid
 self.total_pages = num_pages
 self.allocated_frames = allocated_frames
 self.page_faults = 0
 self.page_references = deque() # (page, timestamp)
 self.working_set = set()
 
 def generate_reference(self, current_time):
 """Generate a page reference using locality model"""
 if random.random() < 0.7: # 70% locality - reference working set
 if self.working_set:
 page = random.choice(list(self.working_set))
 else:
 page = random.randint(0, min(self.total_pages, 5))
 else: # 30% - reference new page
 page = random.randint(0, self.total_pages - 1)
 
 self.page_references.append((page, current_time))
 self.working_set.add(page)
 return page

 def get_page_fault_rate(self, time_window):
 """Calculate page fault rate over time window"""
 current_time = time.time()
 faults_in_window = sum(1 for _, t in self.page_references 
 if current_time - t < time_window)
 return faults_in_window / time_window if time_window > 0 else 0

class ThrashingSimulator:
 def __init__(self, total_frames, time_window=2.0):
 self.total_frames = total_frames
 self.time_window = time_window
 self.processes = []
 
 def add_process(self, process):
 self.processes.append(process)
 
 def detect_thrashing(self):
 """Check if system is thrashing based on page fault rates"""
 total_faults = sum(p.page_faults for p in self.processes)
 avg_fault_rate = total_faults / len(self.processes) if self.processes else 0
 
 # System is thrashing if avg fault rate is very high
 return avg_fault_rate > 5.0 # More than 5 faults per second
 
 def page_fault_frequency_allocation(self):
 """
 Page Fault Frequency (PFF) Algorithm:
 - Increase frames if fault rate too high
 - Decrease frames if fault rate too low
 """
 for p in self.processes:
 fault_rate = p.get_page_fault_rate(self.time_window)
 
 if fault_rate > 3.0: # Too high - need more frames
 if p.allocated_frames < self.total_frames:
 p.allocated_frames += 1
 print(f"Process {p.pid}: High fault rate ({fault_rate:.2f}/s), "
 f"allocated +1 frame → {p.allocated_frames}")
 elif fault_rate < 0.5 and p.allocated_frames > 2: # Too low
 p.allocated_frames -= 1
 print(f"Process {p.pid}: Low fault rate ({fault_rate:.2f}/s), "
 f"allocated -1 frame → {p.allocated_frames}")
 
 def simulate(self, num_references=100):
 """Run thrashing simulation"""
 print(f"=== Thrashing Simulation ===")
 print(f"Total Frames: {self.total_frames}, Time Window: {self.time_window}s\n")
 
 for step in range(num_references):
 current_time = time.time()
 
 # Each process generates a reference
 for p in self.processes:
 page = p.generate_reference(current_time)
 
 # Simulate page fault if page not in allocated frames
 pages_in_memory = min(p.allocated_frames, len(p.working_set))
 if page not in list(p.working_set)[:pages_in_memory]:
 p.page_faults += 1
 
 # Apply PFF every 20 steps
 if step > 0 and step % 20 == 0:
 self.page_fault_frequency_allocation()
 
 if self.detect_thrashing():
 print(f"\n⚠️ THRASHING DETECTED at step {step}!")
 print(f" Average fault rate too high. Consider reducing processes.\n")
 
 # Summary
 print("\n=== Final Results ===")
 for p in self.processes:
 print(f"Process {p.pid}: {p.page_faults} faults, "
 f"{p.allocated_frames} frames, working set size: {len(p.working_set)}")

# Demo: Compare normal vs thrashing conditions
def demo():
 # Scenario 1: Healthy system (enough frames)
 print("\n" + "="*50)
 print("SCENARIO 1: Healthy System (Enough Frames)")
 print("="*50)
 
 sim1 = ThrashingSimulator(total_frames=100)
 sim1.add_process(Process(1, 50, 40)) # Video editor
 sim1.add_process(Process(2, 30, 25)) # Compiler
 sim1.simulate(50)
 
 # Scenario 2: Thrashing (too many processes, too few frames)
 print("\n" + "="*50)
 print("SCENARIO 2: Thrashing System (Over-committed)")
 print("="*50)
 
 sim2 = ThrashingSimulator(total_frames=100)
 sim2.add_process(Process(1, 50, 15)) # Too few frames
 sim2.add_process(Process(2, 30, 15)) # Too few frames
 sim2.add_process(Process(3, 40, 15)) # Too few frames
 sim2.add_process(Process(4, 25, 15)) # Too few frames
 sim2.simulate(50)

if __name__ == "__main__":
 demo()
examples.explanation: This Python simulation demonstrates thrashing by modeling processes with working sets and page references. It implements the Page Fault Frequency (PFF) algorithm which dynamically allocates frames based on fault rates - increasing frames when fault rate is too high and decreasing when too low. The simulation shows how over-committing memory leads to thrashing.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n + w) where w=working set size

examples.title: C: Page Replacement with Working Set Tracking
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating local page replacement and working set tracking to prevent thrashing
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates working set tracking and local page replacement to prevent thrashing. Key features: 1) Working Set calculation tracks pages referenced within a time window, 2) Local LRU replacement ensures a thrashing process only affects its own frames (not stealing from others), 3) The can_admit_process() function implements the Working Set Model admission control to prevent thrashing before it starts.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for working set calculation, O(f) for page lookup where f=allocated frames
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) per process where f=allocated frames
